title: Transformation of competent E. coli
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"d0h1v","text":"Marburg iGEM 2021 Team's Competent E. coli transformation protocol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"140ht","text":"Prepare a container with ice","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"blhmh","text":"Check if there are antibiotic plates ready, otherwise new plates need to be cast","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2s0cj","text":"Heat up the heat block for the heat shock","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"4nlb9","text":"Chemically competent E. coli","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2aqlu","text":"~100 Plasmid DNA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b9itp","text":"LB/SOC Medium","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"18gb3","text":"Antibiotic containing Agar Plate (pre-heat for higher efficiency)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f2qdb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Briefly thaw chemically competent E. coli cells on ice.
Add ∼ 100 ng of plasmid-DNA to the cells. Alternatively just use 1 µL, or 2 µl for retransformations and 5 µL for freshly cloned constructs (Golden Gate reactions).
Incubate on ice for 00:30:00 (can be skipped for retransformations).
Heat shock the cells at 42 °C for 00:00:45 (30 seconds for NEB® Turbo cells).
Recover on ice for 00:05:00 , then add 500 µL of SOC Medium Contributed by users to the cells. Alternatively use LB medium, but SOC is better for the cells.

Incubate at 37 °C for 1 hour (if construct confers amp resistance) or 2 hours (if construct confers kan, cam, tet or spec resistance). Use the incubation time to prepare the plates and pre-warm them in an incubator.
Plate 100 µL on pre-warmed agar plates containing the corresponding antibiotic.
Optionally you can centrifuge for 00:05:00 at 3500 x g, °C, , resuspend the pellet in the remaining ∼ 100 µL and plate it.
creator: yasoo
